items.title: Controversial NT child protection laws to be debated after weeks of criticism

children.content: The Northern Territory parliament is set to debate controversial child protection laws, which have been criticised for their potential impact on the Aboriginal Child Placement Principle.

image.altText: Children on the monkey bars

items.title: PM backs bid for future NT AFL team, no promises on funding

children.content: Anthony Albanese says he would love to see the creation of a Northern Territory AFL team while visiting the Tiwi Islands in the NT.

image.altText: The Tiwi Islands footy team with Anthony Albanese.

items.title: Hundreds march on Pine Gap in secretive site's biggest protest in decades

children.content: About 300 people have marched on Pine Gap in Alice Springs, protesting against its role in US military operations, in the largest protest at the top-secret defence site since 1987.

articles.title: Hundreds gather in Alice Springs ahead of Pine Gap protest

children.content: Hundreds of activists and academics have gathered in Alice Springs to join Arrernte traditional owners ahead of a planned protest against the joint Australian-US military base at Pine Gap.

image.altText: Two older women smile for the camera.

gallery.altText: A crowd of protesters with police watching them.
A large group of  protesters gathered on a wide road, on one side of a roadblock.
A large group of protesters walking down a wide road, holding signs and flags.

thumbnail.altText: A crowd of protesters with police watching them.
A large group of  protesters gathered on a wide road, on one side of a roadblock.
A large group of protesters walking down a wide road, holding signs and flags.

items.title: PM dashes hopes for new NT hospital during $10m maternity announcement

children.content: Prime Minister Anthony Albanese announces $10 million to improve maternity services at Royal Darwin Hospital, but maternity care advocates say the money is unlikely to improve staffing issues.

image.altText: Anthony Albanese at a press conference

items.title: 'It's insane': NT government divides opinion with plan to reduce block sizes

children.content: The Northern Territory government is planning to reduce the minimum block size in low-density suburbs from 800 to 450 square metres, sparking concern from some residents.

image.altText: A housing subdivision with half of the section built

items.title: Aircraft from 15 countries touch down in Darwin ahead of war games

children.content: Military aircraft and aviators from 20 countries land in Darwin ahead of Exercise Pitch Black 2026, with the biennial war games poised to begin.

image.altText: The silhouette of a transport plane outline in the sunrise.

items.title: Early-onset bowel cancer becoming more prevalent as signs are missed

children.content: Georgie Cauchi had blood in her stool, constant pain and was struggling to get out of bed, but still in her 20s doctors told her she was too young to have bowel cancer. When it was finally discovered several years later, the tumour had grown so large it was blocking her entire bowel.

image.altText: A young woman in a white shirt sitting in her living room.

items.title: On the Tiwi Islands, where football is a religion, two teams lift the cup

children.content: The Imalu Tigers and Tapalinga Super Stars are the new premiers of Tiwi Islands football, claiming victory in the men's and women's competitions in electric grand final matches on Sunday.

gallery.altText: A child in a yellow short on the shoulders of a man in a football jersey holding a red football with people gathered around them
The tops of people's heads and arms holding up a large trophy
A young child waves blue and yellow streamers in the air and sticks out their tongue, with people in the background

items.title: Firebombed synagogue community to address royal commission

children.content: The community from Melbourne's Adass Israel Synagogue will provide evidence at the Royal Commission on Antisemitism and Social Cohesion today.

image.altText: Three firefighters outside a building which has orange flames coming from the roof at night.

items.title: Iran-backed Houthis declare maritime blockade on Saudi Arabia

children.content: In a statement, the Houthis' military spokesperson Yahya Saree says the group will enforce the blockade against its "criminal Saudi enemy", effective immediately.

articles.title: US launches new strikes as Trump says Iran will 'pay' for soldiers' deaths

children.content: The United States has launched a new wave of strikes on Iran after identifying two soldiers killed while defending against Iranian missile and drone attacks in Jordan.

image.altText: A young female soldier in army greens in front of a US flag.

gallery.altText: Yahya Sarea stands in a military uniform speaking at a podium.
A satellite view of the Bab el-Mandeb Strait.

avatar.alt: A man in a suit stands in a corridor of parliament house.

items.title: Shared lifestyle might be clue to why some couples have higher dementia risk

children.content: Having a spouse with dementia may put you at a higher risk of developing the condition, a new study finds.

image.altText: Elderly couple holding hands

items.title: Anthony Albanese on the night in parliament he would rather not relive

children.content: From left-wing roots to "radical centre" prime minister, Anthony Albanese reflects on his 30-year career in Parliament House.

image.altText: Anthony Albanese stands in front of an Australian flag in a grey suit and blue tie looking serious

items.title: Stubborn stigma fuelling shocking violence against sex workers in Australia

children.content: A new inquiry into violence against sex workers in Australia and Thailand comes just weeks after the murder of Thai teenager Tunchanok Donhomla, allegedly by an Australian man.

gallery.altText: a woman carries a sign saying 'stop mythologising men who kill sex workers'
A frame of a young Thai girl looking at the camera.

thumbnail.altText: a woman carries a sign saying 'stop mythologising men who kill sex workers'
A frame of a young Thai girl looking at the camera.

items.title: Israel criticises Labor's draft Palestine policy ahead of party conference

children.content: Israel's embassy has released a lengthy complaint ahead of Labor's national conference on the party's proposed position on Israel and Palestine.

image.altText: A white man with glasses speaks at a lectern in front of a blue background

items.title: Six years out from hosting the Olympics, this is where Brisbane stands

children.content: Broken promises and scarcely broken ground. Five years since Brisbane secured the 2032 Olympic Games, this is the state of play.

image.altText: A building windows lit up in BNE 2032 with fireworks in the sky.

items.title: Why 'narcissist' bosses are more likely to oppose work from home

children.content: Research published last month found narcissistic leaders — those with a tendency to be self-centred and entitled — were more likely than others to resist remote work.

gallery.altText: a man gesturing as he sits in front of a laptop

thumbnail.altText: a man gesturing as he sits in front of a laptop

items.title: Quiz: How well do you remember these Australian kids' TV shows?

children.content: What was the name of the tough, mangy cat in The Ferals? And which show featured EC, the faceless doll?

image.altText: Child with faceless doll

items.title: The discipline behind royal wedding cellist's success

children.content: Sheku Kanneh-Mason went viral performing at Prince Harry and Meghan Markle's wedding. He's captivated audiences since and is touring Australia this month.

image.altText: Sheku Kanneh-Mason sits holding a gleaming cello, one hand reaching towards a music stand. There's a grand piano in the room

items.title: Moscow targeted by drones while Russia bombards Kyiv

children.content: Ukraine launches its latest attack on the Russian capital, firing more than 400 drones, while the Kremlin deploys more ballistic missiles against targets across Ukraine.

gallery.altText: A large smoke plume over a blue sky seen from a suburban street.

thumbnail.altText: A large smoke plume over a blue sky seen from a suburban street.

items.title: 'I was in tears': Living without a heater through Canberra's winter

children.content: Barbara Belmonte, an ACT public housing resident, has been waiting weeks for her heater to be repaired amid the sub-zero temperatures of a Canberra winter — and she is not the only one.

image.altText: Barbara Belmont, public housing resident, sits and gestures while talking.

items.title: Man charged with murder of UK politician Ann Widdecombe

children.content: Joshua Kerry, 28, will appear in court on Tuesday, UK time, after the death of the prominent conservative politician in her home earlier in July.

image.altText: An older lady speaks to the media.

items.title: More people are installing home cameras. Will it deter crime?

children.content: More home security cameras than ever before are being installed, but experts say it is not clear whether they are deterring criminals.

image.altText: A white security camera on a shed wall

items.title: Ex-league star Dureau dies at 39 after long cancer battle

children.content: Scott Dureau is remembered as an "exceptional" player and person after losing his life to brain cancer.

image.altText: Scott Dureau gives a thumbs up with his left hand

items.title: 'I love my name': Swans star on having the best name in the AFLW

children.content: Zipporah 'Zippy' Fish announced herself immediately on the AFLW stage with a breakout debut season.

gallery.altText: Zippy Fish of the Swans speaks to the media during an AFLW Draft media opportunity.

thumbnail.altText: Zippy Fish of the Swans speaks to the media during an AFLW Draft media opportunity.

items.title: Taxpayers fork out almost $35,000 for MPs to stay at Queensland parliament

children.content: It has been revealed in budget estimates that 191 total overnight stays were reported by MPs last financial year, up from just 39 the previous year.

image.altText: Queensland Parliament House in Brisbane through its front fence on George Street.

items.title: Major milestone for citizen science as part of nationwide Bug Hunt

children.content: More than 1 million observations of insects, spiders, snails and other invertebrates have been recorded by citizen scientists as part of Bug Hunt Australia since October 2025.

image.altText: Woman in broad-brimmed hat with a small black beetle on her finger.

items.title: Critical beeding breakthrough welcome news for lovers of purple passionfruit

children.content: An unpalatable future has been averted for Australians who love purple-skinned passionfruit, with new research ensuring better prospects for the delicious fruit.

gallery.altText: Farmer holds open split passionfruit while touching his dog's head.
A smiling mother and her two sons holding passionfruit and a wooden spoon up.

thumbnail.altText: Farmer holds open split passionfruit while touching his dog's head.
A smiling mother and her two sons holding passionfruit and a wooden spoon up.

items.title: Underwater gardening helping to restore Tasmania's lost kelp forests

children.content: A new live-in internship program on the Tasman Peninsula is working to repopulate the area's endangered giant kelp forests through underwater gardening.

image.altText: Diver swims through kelp forest.

items.title: Glitter in 'beautiful big nugget of rhino poo' unlocks fertility clues

children.content: Edible glitter is being used to track the reproductive cycles of the two youngest southern white rhinos at Monarto Safari Park.

image.altText: A southern white rhino chewing on hay with vivid blue glitter.

items.title: Quadruplets born in Brisbane 'one of the rarest births in the world'

children.content: A Queensland family has grown by four after an extremely rare pregnancy with identical quadruplets all sharing one placenta.

gallery.altText: An ultrasound image of four fetuses in utero

thumbnail.altText: An ultrasound image of four fetuses in utero
